Preserves spec 091's repeatable static invite gate and credential safety; supersedes conflicting full-admin/shared-token/no-schema assumptions.

scopeProgress.name: Bound Browser-Account Policy And Role/Grant Model
Product-Wide CSRF And Origin Mutation Protection
Role And Grant Acceptance And Global Corpus Gating (Disposable Production)

executionHistory.summary: Reconciled the UX-owned production-session contract for login, session recovery, authenticated shell states, safe return, 401-versus-403 handling, true and filtered empty states, degraded dependencies, logout mutation feedback, responsive and accessible behavior, real-cookie Playwright-visible outcomes, and routed security/design questions. No authentication, cookie, browser or test execution, implementation, evidence, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, or deployment is claimed.

Replaced the initialization design with a code-grounded unified browser-session architecture: explicit credential-to-principal binding and scope grants, audience-bound browser and API PASETO purposes, persisted browser issuance, one request authenticator for legacy and modern middleware, revocation-backed logout, fail-closed migration, privacy-safe observability, real-cookie Playwright strategy, and narrow non-destructive rollback. No spec, scope, report, user validation, scenario manifest, source, test, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, or deployment change is claimed.

Reconciled SR-002/SR-003/GRILL-03/SR-006/GRILL-12: BUG-070 explicitly amends certified specs 070/091, preserves the repeatable static invite gate, supersedes conflicting full-admin/shared-token/no-schema assumptions, defines one claim-bound session, daily/operator 2xx/403 acceptance, product-wide CSRF/Origin protection, and global-corpus grants. Preserved SCN-070-001-01..08 and added 09..11. No certified predecessor, source, test, design, scope, report, user validation, certification, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

Planning reconciliation to the analyst-updated spec.md (which added SCN-070-001-09 CSRF/Origin, SCN-070-001-10 roles, and SCN-070-001-11 global-corpus grant-gating after the 2026-07-24T01:27Z planning artifacts were authored). Corrected the stale SCN-070-001-09 middleware-parity mislabel across scopes.md, scenario-manifest.json, and test-plan.json; split planning from 5 to 6 scopes (new SCOPE-05 product-wide CSRF/Origin; SCOPE-06 = former disposable-acceptance scope expanded to cover SCN-01/10/11); re-pointed SCOPE-03 rows to SCN-06/07 and made the AUTH-015 single-authoritative-session-contract an explicit SCOPE-03 Core Outcome to resolve the auth predecessor-amendment consistency finding; restored Test-Plan-row/DoD-test-item parity (5 rows / 5 items per scope, 30 rows total); rebuilt certification.scopeProgress to 6 scopes. Residuals routed to bubbles.design: RF-070-001-01 (design Security relies on SameSite/Origin vs spec AUTH-011 session-bound anti-CSRF proof) and RF-070-001-02 (design Testing table stops at SCN-08 and omits SCN-09/10/11). No source, test, spec, design, bug, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, or deployment change is claimed; status remains in_progress.

Resolved the two design residuals routed by the plan reconciliation. RF-070-001-01 (spec AUTH-011 vs design Security conflict): took option (a) and specified a concrete session-bound anti-CSRF mechanism in a new design.md section '## Cross-Site Request Forgery Protection' — a stateless signed double-submit proof bound to the browser-session PASETO jti/sub/aud via HMAC under a dedicated fail-loud csrf_signing_key (no schema, no per-request DB read), layered on a trusted-Origin check, delivered through a non-authenticating smackerel_csrf companion cookie plus a _csrf form field and a csrf-token meta tag, echoed via _csrf/X-CSRF-Token, enforced for every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE by a MutationTrustGuard across server-form/HTMX/PWA/JSON/Cards/admin with a pre-session variant for login/registration/logout. Reconciled the contradictory 'No token is exposed for a custom CSRF scheme' statement to necessary-not-sufficient SameSite plus a required session-bound proof, and clarified the proof is not session material so the HttpOnly session PASETO stays script-inaccessible. RF-070-001-02: extended the design Testing And Validation Strategy table to SCN-070-001-09/10/11. Supporting internal-consistency edits: MutationTrustGuard foundation contract, foundation-owned CSRF behavior, anti-CSRF proof-carrier variation axis, companion-cookie cross-reference, a 403-before-mutation failure-mapping row, a csrf telemetry line, two rejected-alternative subsections, and a complexity-tracking row. AUTH-011 was upheld (not weakened); the mechanism matches the already-planned SCOPE-05 surface (MutationGuard, csrf middleware/enforcement test files, and the origin_rejected|csrf_missing|csrf_stale|csrf_mismatch|accepted plus family telemetry vocabulary), so no scope/test/DoD re-reconciliation is required. No spec, scope, report, user validation, scenario manifest, source, test, certification, status transition, commit, push, build, or deployment change is claimed; status remains in_progress.

statusDiscipline.scopePickupRule: All dependsOn scopes must be Done. Pick the lowest-numbered eligible scope.
statusDiscipline.specDoneRequires: All scopes done, certification.completedScopes contains every scope ID, and bubbles.validate certifies promotion.

notes: Owner routes required: bubbles.ux for user flows, bubbles.design for root-cause design, and bubbles.plan for final scope/test planning. BUG-070-001 blocks modern authenticated API/PWA Playwright and product journey acceptance.
